title: What To Do When Filler Lasts Too Long In A Client
filename: What To Do When Filler Lasts Too Long In A Client.en.vtt
excerpt: Hi everyone runs with me today and it's a clinical day which is usually really jam-packed if I'm honest but and with less than no time to spare but I currently have a bit of spare time because of what just happened which I wanted to share with you guys because there's some intere…

segments.text: hi everyone runs with me today and it's a clinical day which is usually really jam-packed if I'm honest but and with less than no time to spare but I currently have a bit of spare time because of what just happened which I wanted to share with you guys because there's some interesting learning in it and basically what it was a patient I saw three years and one month ago who loved her result in her mind it was just a perfect experience she wanted to come and basically top-up the area that she had treated and so that she got what she came for the first time again which is great however when I looked at her I realized straight away that I couldn't repeat the procedure it's because she's one of these super long and lasting people with dermal filler and she basically hadn't lost any volume since her treatment it was a non-surgical rhinoplasty and she's consented for me to show you the pictures they're cropped so I'll show you those in a minute but the

interesting thing is why did why does filler last a really long time in some people and is that always a good thing because actually I've realized quite a long time I realize it isn't always good mainly it's good so if you're someone who happens to get a long time one of your fillers you're probably just lucky and it will stay that way but there are certain parts of the face where it doesn't always suit and you just worth being worth knowing about that as a clinician so that you can make sure that you guide your patients which is what I did today which is why I haven't done a retreatment on her in fact we decided after a lengthy chat to actually highlight the procedure and I'll show you why in a second and here are my thoughts on why some people just seem to last a lot longer than others I mean there's lots of variables as it is with all these things but one of them is the M it can be your own metabolism and that means your own enzyme so we actually all have natural hydron days in our tissues we all have different amounts of it so some people will have more of it and some people have less of it and that affects how long the any procedure lasts

there are a few percentage of people who break it down in a matter of weeks they're really disappointing to treat and the biggest one I've ever had was I did a 12 min treatment on her look great a month later gone so because of the way I think about adding value is a really important part this is I don't like to I was and there's no way I was going to make a profit out of that so and we looked after in that way but very disappointing for me too very expensive when that kind of thing happens you are getting for it but on the other end of the spectrum there are people who just don't seem to have much of this enzyme at all and it will just last and my theory is there's some degradation of the product but just a little bit of it a bit of it almost maybe it's time related and as these long-chain dermal fillers break down they form smaller pregnant fragments and any sugar which is what hyaluronic acid dermal fillers are that is the small fragment is more soluble and bigger fragments that's why you know glucose is really soluble and the starch and a

potato for example is solid is it so they're all the similar molecule just in different length chains so those and those little chains I think naturally are more hydrophilic so as filler breaks down if it happens very slowly you get increased water attraction and if it happens slowly enough then patients don't even notice it and they just think it's sometimes they quite you know it might be part of the face where it's actually not a bad thing because you're just retaining volume but in sensitive parts of the face like noses tear drops sometimes lips you lose definition and you add volume and that's exactly what I saw on his patient today so she'd had one mill of a loom at three years ago very happy the result came back because she'd noticed a little dip in her nose return but actually I think what she was seeing was the transition point between filler that was actually getting a little bit fuller and her normal no structure and let me show you what it looks like so and if you can see on this image the gap between here and here is significantly more this is the after

picture so I had done a non-surgical rhinoplasty on her she was really happy with that result thrilled with it we left with her looking like this and three years later she came back looking like that with this extra wide bridge so I think that's this is something really wealth worth knowing about I'll post that picture underneath as well it's really worth knowing about and so that you maybe can build into your consent process that if you're one of the people who lasts a very long time it might be better for you to have a retreatment and a possible possibly reverse it or massage it could be on the cards it's not to say we promised this it'll happens anyone because actually is quite rare it's very rare actually but it's worth knowing about and then you can make sure you get a really good result because if I just retweeted her all right this is my fear actually I think it probably happened down the country people will retreat and they'll try and you know that hold saying if all your tools if the only tool you have is a hammer then all your problems look like a nail it can be a bit like that with

dermal filler someone comes in they want to read treatment they want something better you end up putting more filler into that and you are gonna disappoint yourself and most importantly you're gonna disappoint your patients so think about sometimes years after treatment it might be worth putting a little bit of high layers in to take that product out or at least a very vigorous massage or routine of massaging at home is another option but I'm interested to hear your thoughts what other people think about this do you think we should be starting with a clean slate every three years if you've had a treatment it lasts and my concern is if you're doing bigger treatments and this is happening you know we need to refer be referring back to photographs and just checking that the volume is definitely gone and you're not trying to get definition back on top of a hypervolemic state because the filler is breaking down slowly because that's just a recipe for pillow face basically so anyway that's my thought for today let me know what you think and I will try and answer any questions that come back tomorrow but it just thought was quite an interesting

thing we don't often think about what happens when filler just lasts and lasts it isn't always a good thing in terms of the aesthetic result although she's obviously had at least two years of it being good I think of the last year got slightly worse and a little bit puffy so let me know what you think and we think this is useful and tag someone who might find it interesting or you can share it or and most of all I remember I love reading the comments and if you've got other questions or things you're wondering about this topic or any cop any topic you can message me or write in the comments and I really appreciate that so thanks for watching I'll see you guys probably tomorrow I'm just gonna wait goodbye bye guys thanks for watching
